problem.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
problem.description: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming
solutions.description: Define dp[i] as the length of the longest increasing subsequence ending at index i, initialized to 1. For each element, scan all previous elements and extend any subsequence whose last element is smaller. The answer is the maximum value in dp. The O(n²) approach is straightforward; an O(n log n) variant using binary search on a patience-sorting tails array is also possible.
